result.meeting_context: This is a Finance Committee meeting to review the annual financial audit results and comprehensive financial report for fiscal year 2021-2022. The committee received presentations from the external auditor and city staff, discussed audit findings, and made recommendations to City Council.

points.speaker: Sally Lieber
points.text: Committee approved February 28th, 2022 meeting minutes unanimously via roll call vote
points.source: Sally Lieber : OK, all in favor, say aye... Unanimous

points.speaker: Jesse Takahashi
points.text: MGO has served as external auditor for seven years, completing annual financial audit per city charter Section 1106 requirement
points.source: Jesse Takahashi : Mgos been the external auditor for the city for the last seven years now

points.speaker: Jesse Takahashi
points.text: General operating fund finished fiscal year 2022 with $17.5 million operating balance
points.source: Jesse Takahashi : the general operating fund finished the year, fiscal year 2022 with a 17 and a half million dollar operating balance

points.speaker: Banjamin Lau
points.text: City received clean audit opinion on financial statements with no findings on internal control over financial reporting
points.source: Banjamin Lau : We did not identify any findings on internal control over financial reporting for this year

points.speaker: Banjamin Lau
points.text: City successfully implemented GASB Statement 87 lease accounting standard, creating $800 million lease receivable with offsetting deferred inflow
points.source: Banjamin Lau : your balance sheet itself has recorded a lease receivable of roughly about 800 million with an offsetting deferred inflow of resources

points.speaker: Banjamin Lau
points.text: Large lease receivable balance primarily relates to leases from major tech firm within the city
points.source: Banjamin Lau : it's mainly related to a few leases for one of your gigantic tech firm that is within your city

topics.topic: Equipment Maintenance and Replacement Fund Analysis

points.speaker: Lucas Ramirez
points.text: Equipment maintenance and replacement fund has $26 million balance, significantly higher than historical expenditure patterns over past 15 years
points.source: Lucas Ramirez : the fund balance for the equipment maintenance and replacement fund is $26 million

points.speaker: Jesse Takahashi
points.text: Fire apparatus replacement was unusual one-time expenditure, with partial payment made and remaining deliveries expected over 1-2 years
points.source: Jesse Takahashi : we have made a partial payment, but that's going to be overtime because I think it's going to take about somewhere between one and two years to actually receive all of those

points.speaker: Jesse Takahashi
points.text: Staff will review equipment replacement fund charges to determine if rates should be adjusted for more reasonable balance
points.source: Jesse Takahashi : that's something that we, we, we certainly can look at and you know get get back to the committee with an update

points.speaker: Jesse Takahashi
points.text: Community benefit funds are tracked through city financial system sub-ledgers but not separately reported in ACFR document
points.source: Jesse Takahashi : we do have a a mechanism for tracking those community benefit funds but but it's not done within say the the ACT for

points.speaker: Lisa Matichak
points.text: Some community benefits don't flow through city finances when developers directly provide subsidized rent or infrastructure improvements
points.source: Lisa Matichak : Most of the community benefits are subsidizing rent, building parking spaces, and building out the infrastructure

points.speaker: Lisa Matichak
points.text: Staff will update organizational chart to change 'Mountain View citizens' to 'Mountain View residents' and add Public Safety Advisory Board
points.source: Lisa Matichak : Instead of having Mountain View citizens, it would be Mountain Dew residents

points.speaker: Jesse Takahashi
points.text: Staff will work on reformatting ACFR for electronic delivery rather than print format to improve readability
points.source: Jesse Takahashi : we'll definitely get to work on trying to reformat for electronic age

points.speaker: Banjamin Lau
points.text: Single audit for federal compliance not yet completed but expected to meet March 31, 2023 statutory deadline
points.source: Banjamin Lau : we expect to meet the statutory deadline of March 31st, 2023

points.speaker: Lucas Ramirez
points.text: Single audit will include American Rescue Plan Act funds as federal funding subject to compliance review
points.source: Lucas Ramirez : the single the audit will include the American Rescue Plan Act funds since those are federal funds

points.speaker: Banjamin Lau
points.text: Auditor thanked city finance team led by Jesse Takahashi, Grace Zheng, and Janet for continuous support during audit process
points.source: Banjamin Lau : I would like to thank city staff for continuous support

points.speaker: Lucas Ramirez
points.text: Committee members thanked Jesse Takahashi for service to city as this was his final Finance Committee meeting
points.source: Lucas Ramirez : I'm sorry that this will be Jesse's last meeting with us. Thank you very much for your service to the city

decisions.text: Staff will review equipment replacement fund charges and provide committee update on potential rate adjustments for more reasonable balance
decisions.source: Jesse Takahashi : that's something that we, we, we certainly can look at and you know get get back to the committee with an update in terms of maybe where we we can go with that

decisions.text: Staff will update ACFR organizational chart to change 'Mountain View citizens' to 'Mountain View residents' and add Public Safety Advisory Board
decisions.source: Jesse Takahashi : we'll we'll get that corrected

decisions.text: Staff will reformat ACFR document for electronic delivery rather than print format to improve readability
decisions.source: Jesse Takahashi : we'll definitely get to work on trying to reformat for electronic age

decisions.text: Committee unanimously approved recommendation to City Council to accept the Annual Comprehensive Financial Report and auditor's reports
decisions.source: Lucas Ramirez : I'm happy to to move the staff recommendation... Victoria labrador: Unanimous
